2603.19918.title: Learning Like Humans: Analogical Concept Learning for Generalized Category Discovery
2603.19918.paper_summary: Generalized Category Discovery (GCD) seeks to uncover novel categories in unlabeled data while preserving recognition of known categories, yet prevailing visual-only pipelines and the loose coupling between supervised learning and discovery often yield brittle boundaries on fine-grained, look-alike categories. We introduce the Analogical Textual Concept Generator (ATCG), a plug-and-play module that analogizes from labeled knowledge to new observations, forming textual concepts for unlabeled samples. Fusing these analogical textual concepts with visual features turns discovery into a visual-textual reasoning process, transferring prior knowledge to novel data and sharpening category separation. ATCG attaches to both parametric and clustering style GCD pipelines and requires no changes to their overall design. Across six benchmarks, ATCG consistently improves overall, known-class, and novel-class performance, with the largest gains on fine-grained data. Our code is available at: https://github.com/zhou-9527/AnaLogical-GCD.
2603.19918.paper_summary_zh: 一般化類別發現 (GCD) 旨在揭示未標記數據中的新類別，同時保留對已知類別的識別，然而現有的僅視覺管道以及監督學習與發現之間的鬆散耦合，往往在細粒度、相似類別上產生脆弱的邊界。我們介紹了類比文本概念生成器 (ATCG)，這是一個即插即用的模塊，能夠從標記知識類比到新的觀察，為未標記樣本形成文本概念。將這些類比文本概念與視覺特徵融合，使發現變成一個視覺-文本推理過程，將先前的知識轉移到新數據上並加強類別分離。ATCG 可以附加到參數化和聚類風格的 GCD 管道上，並且不需要改變它們的整體設計。在六個基準測試中，ATCG 始終提高了整體、已知類別和新類別的性能，尤其在細粒度數據上獲得了最大的增益。我們的代碼可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/zhou-9527/AnaLogical-GCD。
2603.19918.author: Jizhou Han et.al.
2603.19918.authors: Jizhou Han, Chenhao Ding, Yuhang He, Qiang Wang, Shaokun Wang, SongLin Dong, Yihong Gong
